perturbation.original_text: 2. The Principal grants Distributor non- exclusive rights to sell these products within the countries of Romania (the "Territory"), which may be amended by mutual written agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Principal grants Distributor non- exclusive rights to sell these products within the countries of Romania (the "Territory"). This territory is exclusive, and shall not be amended without the express written consent of both parties and payment of a fee determined solely by the Principal.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the territory is non-exclusive and can be amended by mutual agreement. The change makes the territory exclusive, contradicts the non-exclusive nature, and adds the condition for a fee determined solely by the Principal, which goes against the "mutual agreement" aspect. It contradicts the previous clause in the same section that mentions this territory as non-exclusive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Principal grants Distributor non- exclusive rights to sell these products within the countries of Romania (the "Territory"). This territory is exclusive, and shall not be amended without the express written consent of both parties and payment of a fee determined solely by the Principal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the clause. The original text specifies a "non-exclusive" territory that "may be amended by mutual written agreement." The changed text makes the territory "exclusive" and adds a fee determined solely by the Principal, nullifying the initial non-exclusive aspect and the mutual agreement component. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text is the same, the change creates a contradiction because the contract now simultaneously states the territory is both exclusive and non-exclusive, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement shall be in effect until March 18. 2021, unless sooner terminated by either party upon (30) days written notice, without cause.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall be in effect until March 18. 2021, unless sooner terminated by either party upon (30) days written notice. The Principal, however, can terminate the contract immediately, without notice or cause, if the Distributor fails to meet sales targets defined at the Principal's sole discretion at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination by either party with 30 days' notice without cause. The added clause allows the Principal to terminate immediately without notice if sales targets, defined at the Principal's sole discretion, are not met. This contradicts the original 30-day notice requirement and the 'without cause' condition for the Principal. It contradicts the term "unless sooner terminated by either party upon (30) days written notice, without cause."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be in effect until March 18. 2021, unless sooner terminated by either party upon (30) days written notice. The Principal, however, can terminate the contract immediately, without notice or cause, if the Distributor fails to meet sales targets defined at the Principal's sole discretion at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the Principal to terminate the agreement immediately without notice or cause under specific conditions (failure to meet sales targets), which directly contradicts the original clause allowing either party to terminate with 30 days' written notice 'without cause.' This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the Principal now has two conflicting options for termination. The modified clause undermines the 30-day notice provision and the 'without cause' condition, making the terms regarding termination inconsistent and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 13. The Distributor is an independent contractor and nothing contained in this agreement shall be deemed or interpreted to constitute the Distributor as a partner or employee of the Principal, nor shall either party have any authority to bind the other in any respect, it being understood and agreed that all orders submitted by the Distributor are subject to acceptance by Principal in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. The Distributor is an independent contractor and nothing contained in this agreement shall be deemed or interpreted to constitute the Distributor as a partner or employee of the Principal, nor shall either party have any authority to bind the other in any respect, except that the Distributor is required to follow all direct instructions from the Principal regarding marketing and sales strategies. All orders submitted by the Distributor are subject to acceptance by Principal in its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Distributor is an independent contractor without the authority to bind the Principal. The added clause that the Distributor is required to follow all direct instructions from the Principal regarding marketing and sales strategies erodes the independence of the contractor, implying a level of control that is more akin to an employee-employer relationship, therefore, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction lies in the phrase "The Distributor is an independent contractor", which is undermined by the requirement to follow the Principal's instructions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. The Distributor is an independent contractor and nothing contained in this agreement shall be deemed or interpreted to constitute the Distributor as a partner or employee of the Principal, nor shall either party have any authority to bind the other in any respect, except that the Distributor is required to follow all direct instructions from the Principal regarding marketing and sales strategies. All orders submitted by the Distributor are subject to acceptance by Principal in its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that the Distributor is 'required to follow all direct instructions from the Principal regarding marketing and sales strategies'. This directly contradicts the earlier statement in the same clause that 'nothing contained in this agreement shall be deemed or interpreted to constitute the Distributor as a partner or employee of the Principal, nor shall either party have any authority to bind the other in any respect'. The requirement to follow direct instructions implies a level of control and authority typically associated with an employer-employee relationship, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding the Distributor's status as an independent contractor. The contradictory language is present in the same section (Section 13) which makes the contradiction more apparent.
